operation: First, a 38mm radius circle is sketched on the XY workplane and extruded 38mm along the Z-axis to form a solid base cylinder. The topmost (highest Z) circular edge of the cylinder is filleted with a 3.0mm radius, while the bottommost (lowest Z) circular edge receives a 3.0mm chamfer. Next, a 32mm radius circle is sketched on the XY workplane and extruded 28mm along the Z-axis to create a smaller cylindrical cutting body, which is then subtracted from the original larger cylinder to produce the final hollow model.
geometry: This is a short, hollow concentric cylindrical cap closed at the top and open at the bottom. Its inner cylindrical bore does not extend all the way through the part, leaving a solid flat upper surface. The outer top circumferential edge is smoothly rounded, while the outer bottom circumferential edge has an angled bevel, with all cylindrical features aligned along the same central vertical axis.
function: This is a protective fitting cap designed to fit over the end of a cylindrical pipe, post, or other mating cylindrical component. Its rounded top removes sharp edges for safe handling, and the bottom bevel simplifies alignment and installation onto the part it is intended to cover.
